signals.title: AUD/GBP — G8 Bilateral Theme + Political Uncertainty
signals.text: AUD/GBP exhibits a 1.284% G8 performance spread as GBP tumbles -0.92% intraday following reports of political uncertainty. AUD remains the G8 leader (+0.652% avg) despite a -60bp carry disadvantage vs USD, while GBP (-0.632% avg) breaks below its 100 and 200-day MAs. The divergence is driven by idiosyncratic GBP weakness rather than broad risk-on sentiment, as VIX remains at 17.3 (normal volatility).

signals.title: USD/JPY — Positioning + Options + Yields
signals.text: USD/JPY at 158.253 supported by +287bp OIS carry (SOFR 3.60% vs TONA 0.73%) as US Retail Sales outperformance drives DXY to two-week highs. However, 25d RR at -1.69 (Saxo) reveals a strong institutional put skew, signaling heavy demand for downside protection despite COT LF net short -61,340 (crowded carry). The correlation break (z=-1.87σ) vs US 10Y suggests the pair is becoming less sensitive to yield upside as intervention fears or BoJ hike bets (16 Jun) cap the advance.

signals.title: NZD/USD — COT vs OIS Divergence + RBNZ
signals.text: NZD/USD COT LF net short -19,294 (specs fading the CB) vs RBNZ hike bias priced for 27 May. With NZD long% at an extreme low of 18.5% and retail 70% long, a hawkish RBNZ delivery creates significant short-squeeze risk. NZD/USD at 0.5914 remains pressured by a +150bp USD carry advantage, but crowded spec shorts are vulnerable if the RBNZ signals a terminal rate hike.

signals.title: AUD/USD — Volatility + Retail Divergence
signals.text: AUD/USD IV at 14.5% vs HV30 at 10.2% generates a +4.3% Vol Risk Premium, signaling elevated hedging demand. This aligns with a sharp divergence where retail is 82% short while COT LF net positioning is +58,994 long (78.3% long). Institutional conviction remains bullish despite the intraday -0.48% dip to 0.7223; retail capitulation risk remains high if price holds the 0.7200 support level.

signals.title: USD/CHF — Volatility Premium + Carry
signals.text: USD/CHF at 0.7833 supported by a massive +375bp carry advantage (Fed 3.75% vs SNB 0.0%). Options market is pricing extreme tail risk with IV at 12.8% vs HV30 7.1% (VRP +5.7%), the highest in the G10 space. While retail is 76% long, the elevated vol premium suggests professional desks are paying up for protection against a rapid CHF safe-haven reversal despite the carry-positive environment.
